California
Fair P01itical
Practices Commission
May 9, 19 91
Mr. Roger A. Brown
Larr, Offices of Lawrence A. Haun
Eleven and One-HaIf South washington
P. O. Box 1056
Sonora, CA 9537 O
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-91-0 3 3
Dear Mr. Brown:
This is in response to your request for advice under the
revolving door provisions of the PoLitical Reform Act (the
'tAct't).1 
Your Ietter presented three questions, two of which may
not appropriately be ansv/ered here.z
OUEST ION
Do the revolving door provisions of the Act prohibit you from
representing respondents in Fair PoIitical Practices Commission
Enforcenent Case No. 88/239 (the rrcaserr) ?
1 Go,r"rn..nt code sections 81ooo-91015. AII statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulatj.ons Section
18ooo, et ses. Al,l references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the catifornia code of Regulations.
)' 
one of your questions requested a continuance of a ProbabLe
cause conference on behalf of respondents to a current Fair
Political Practices Commission enforcement action. 
Since you do
not represent those respondents, it would be improper to answer
that question here. Another of your questions is whether the
Cornmission would agree to forbear fron moving your
disqualification in Fair Political Practj.ces commission
Enforcement case No. 88/2f9.3 we believe it woul.d be
inappropriate to analyze that issue here.
428 J Street, Suite 8O0 . P.O. Box 807 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916) 322-'>660


Our File No. A-9■
-033
Page 2
CONCLUS ION
As a former supervisor, you are deemed to have "participated"
in the case during your tenure as Enforcement Division chief for
the Fair Political Practices Cornmission (the rrCornmission'r) .
Therefore, the revolving door provisions of the Act prohibit your
representation of respondents in the case.
FACTS
You hrere formerly enployed by the Commission as chief of the
Enforcement Division. 
You resigned as division chief as of July
1988, and left the Commission in october l-988.
on or about June 9, 1988, the Commission Enforcement Division
received the complaint that initiated the case at issue. The
Enforcement Division acknowledged receipt of the complaint by form
Letter on or about June 14, 1988. Your Ietter states that you
have no recollection of the case, and that you have no
confidential information about the case by virtue of your former
emp I oyment .
You now wish to represent the respondents in the case. You
.:re questioning whether the specific requirements of Section 87401
would prohibit you from undertaking such representation.
ANALYSIS
The language of Sections 87400 and 87401 is quite specific in
connection with employment that a former state adninistrative
off iciaLis 
prohi.bited from accepting.
Section 87401 sets forth the following:
No former state administrative official, 
after
the terrnination of his or her enp).oyment or term of
office, shall for cornpensation act as agent or
attorney for, or otherwise represent, any other
person (other than the State of California) before
any court or state adrninistrative agency or any
officer or enployee thereof by making any fornal or
informal appearance, or by naking any oral or
l.rritten communication with the intent to influence,
in connection with any judicial, quasi-judicial or
other proceeding if both of the following apply:
(a) The state of Cal-ifornia is a party or has
a direct and substantial interest.
(b) The proceeding is one in which the forrner
state administrative official 
participated.
In the circumstance posed by your letter, the requirernent
stated j.n subdivision (a) would be met because the Conmission, an
administrative agency of the State of California, is a party to
the case. Subdivision (b) then requires that you have
"participated" in the same "proceeding" while a former 'rstate


Our File No. A-9■
-033
Page 3
administrative of f icial.'r 
As a former commission division chief,
you were a state administrative official,. 
(Section 87400(b).)
The proceeding in this situat.ion, a quasi-judicial rnatter
involving specific parties conducted by a state administrative
agency. meets the definition of "proceedingI set forth in Section
87400 (c) . r
The remaining question is whether or not you I'participated"
in the proceedings. Section 87400(d), defines participation as
foI Iows :
It Partic ipatedtr neans to have taken part
personally and substantially through decision,
approval, disapproval, formal written
reconmendation, rendering advice on a substantial
basis, investj-gation or use of confidential
information as an officer or employee, but
excluding approval, disapproval or rendering of
legal advisory opinions to departmental or agency
staff which do not involve a specif j-c party or
parties.
Several prior advice letters have dealt with the issue of
participation as defined by section 874oo(d). Since 1985. staff
has consistently advised that a former of f i.cial of a state
adr,rinistrative agency was qEEEe.d to have participated in all
proceedings of his former agency if he was in the agency's
supervisory chain for the proceedings during the time he was
employed by the agency. (See Blonien Advice Letter, No. A-89-463,
and Sanford Advice letter, No. A-85-182, copies enclosed. ) 
The
comnission at its April 25, 1991 meeting considered your request
for advice, and, in response to that request, passed a motion
deeming former suPervisors in state adrninistrative agencies to
have pirticipated personally and substantial'Iy in proceedings
which were initiated or were pending, and vrere under the forner
official's 
supervisory authority, during the prior state
employrnent. Since the subject case is a proceedinq initiated
auiinq your tenure as Enf;rcement Division chief, under sections
gz.loo-and 87401 you are prohibited fron representing respondents
in Enforcenent case No. 88/239.
3 section 87400(c) defines a proceeding to inctude any
"proceeding, application, request for a ruling or other.
oLtermination,-contract, clain, controversy, investigation,
;;;;;;; 
accusation, arrest or other particular matter invorving a
=p..ifi" party or parties in any court or state administrative
ug.ncy, inctuaing Lut not Iinited to any proceeding governed by
;;;;a;; - 1.o...""ing with section 11s00) of Division 3 of ritle 
2
of the Government Code. "


Our Flle No. A-91-033
Page 4
If you have questions regarding the aboVe′
 p■ease do not
hesitate to COntact me by telephone at (9■
6)322-590■
.
Sincere■y′
SCOTT HALLABRIN
Acting General Counse■
